query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 410d8849).

## Issue to Resolve

Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Autoformatting should not reformat below line to incorrect state
```
private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Observed Behavior
Autoformatting moves `DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))` to the next line and as the results it looks like this:
```
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = 
    DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Autoformatting should allow to reproduce this. My Detekt config:
```
formatting:
    excludes: [ '**/build/**' ]
    MaximumLineLength:
        active: false
    NoBlankLineBeforeRbrace:
        active: false
    Indentation:
        active: false
    ArgumentListWrapping:
        active: false
    MultiLineIfElse:
        active: false
```

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.1
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used: Detekt 1.23.0. Related issue https://github.com/detekt/detekt/issues/6223
* Version of Gradle: 7.3.3
* Kotlin version 1.8.21

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 26→import mu.KotlinLogging
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→
 29→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }

130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",

166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}
169→

 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig

 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY

 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe

 28→import mu.KotlinLogging
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 30→
 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a value parameter.
 35→ *
 36→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 37→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 38→ * indentation rule is not run.
 39→ *
 40→ * This rule has many similarities with the [PropertyWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 41→ */
 42→public class ParameterWrappingRule :
 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 45→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 48→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 49→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private var line = 1
 53→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        line = 1
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 72→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 82→
 83→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 84→
 85→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 86→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→        val fromNode =
 88→            node
 89→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 90→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 91→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 92→                ?: node
 93→
 94→        node
 95→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 96→            ?.let { colon ->
 97→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 98→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 99→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
100→                    return
101→                }
102→            }
103→
104→        node
105→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
106→            ?.let { typeReference ->
107→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
108→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                    return
110→                }
111→            }
112→
113→        node
114→            .findChildByType(EQ)
115→            ?.let { equal ->
116→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
117→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
118→                    return
119→                }
120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
146→        node: ASTNode,
147→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→    ) {
150→        emit(
151→            node.startOffset - 1,
152→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
153→            true,
154→        )
155→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
156→        if (autoCorrect) {
157→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
166→    ) {
167→        emit(
168→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
169→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
170→            true,
171→        )
172→        LOGGER.trace {
173→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
174→        }
175→        if (autoCorrect) {
176→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
177→        }
178→    }
179→}
180→
181→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.EOL_CHAR
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
  7→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  8→
  9→internal class PropertyWrappingRuleTest {
 10→    private val propertyWrappingRuleAssertThat = KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { PropertyWrappingRule() }
 11→
 12→    @Test
 13→    fun `Given that the variable name and the following colon do not fit on the same line as val or var keyword then wrap after the colon`() {
 14→        val code =
 15→            """
 16→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER     $EOL_CHAR
 17→            val aVariableWithALooooooongName: String
 18→            """.trimIndent()
 19→        val formattedCode =
 20→            """
 21→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER     $EOL_CHAR
 22→            val aVariableWithALooooooongName:
 23→                String
 24→            """.trimIndent()
 25→        propertyWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 26→            .setMaxLineLength()
 27→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
 28→            .hasLintViolation(2, 34, "Missing newline after \":\"")
 29→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    @Test
 33→    fun `Given that the type does not fit on the same line as the variable name`() {
 34→        val code =
 35→            """
 36→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
 37→            val aVariableWithALongerName: TypeWithALongName
 38→            val aVariableWithALongName2: TypeWithALongName
 39→            """.trimIndent()
 40→        val formattedCode =
 41→            """
 42→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
 43→            val aVariableWithALongerName:
 44→                TypeWithALongName
 45→            val aVariableWithALongName2: TypeWithALongName
 46→            """.trimIndent()
 47→        propertyWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 48→            .setMaxLineLength()
 49→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
 50→            .hasLintViolation(2, 30, "Missing newline before \"TypeWithALongName\"")
 51→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    @Test
 55→    fun `Given that equals sign before the value does not fit on the same line as the type and variable name then wrap after the equals sign`() {
 56→        val code =
 57→            """
 58→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                     $EOL_CHAR
 59→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongerName = TypeWithALongName(123)
 60→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongName2 = TypeWithALongName(123)
 61→            """.trimIndent()
 62→        val formattedCode =
 63→            """
 64→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                     $EOL_CHAR
 65→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongerName =
 66→                TypeWithALongName(123)
 67→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongName2 =
 68→                TypeWithALongName(123)
 69→            """.trimIndent()
 70→        propertyWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 71→            .setMaxLineLength()
 72→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
 73→            .hasLintViolations(
 74→                LintViolation(2, 50, "Missing newline after \"=\""),
 75→                LintViolation(3, 49, "Missing newline before \"TypeWithALongName(123)\""),
 76→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    @Test
 80→    fun `Given that the value does not fit on the same line as the type and variable name then wrap after the equals sign`() {
 81→        val code =
 82→            """
 83→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                                            $EOL_CHAR
 84→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongerName = TypeWithALongName(123)
 85→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongName2 = TypeWithALongName(123)
 86→            """.trimIndent()
 87→        val formattedCode =
 88→            """
 89→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                                            $EOL_CHAR
 90→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongerName =
 91→                TypeWithALongName(123)
 92→            val aVariableWithALongName: TypeWithALongName2 = TypeWithALongName(123)
 93→            """.trimIndent()
 94→        propertyWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 95→            .setMaxLineLength()
 96→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
 97→            .hasLintViolation(2, 50, "Missing newline before \"TypeWithALongName(123)\"")
 98→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 99→    }
100→}
101→

 27→// When system and/or environment variables below are enabled, the unit tests provide additional logging information.
 28→private const val KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE = "KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE" // Keep value in sync with value in 'logback-test.xml'
 29→
 30→@Suppress("unused")
 31→private val LOGGER =
 32→    KotlinLogging
 33→        .logger {}
 34→        .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
 35→            if (!logger.isTraceEnabled || !logger.isDebugEnabled) {
 36→                logger.info {
 37→                    """
 38→                    Additional information can be printed during running of unit tests, by setting environment variable below:
 39→                        $KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE=[on|off]
 40→                    """.trimIndent()
 41→                }
 42→            }
 43→        }.initKtLintKLogger()
 44→
 45→// The execution of the unit tests may not be affected by the ".editorconfig" configuration of the Ktlint project itself. So each unit test
 46→// is to be run on a KtlintTestFileSystem not having any ".editorconfig" files. This variable should not be exposed, as unit tests should
 47→// not be allowed to write any content on the file system.
 48→// As this FileSystem is not modified it is not considered to be a problem that the file system is not closed after each unit test.
 49→private val KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM = KtlintTestFileSystem()
 50→
 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74→    /**
 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**
 79→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126→
127→        return this
128→    }
129→
130→    /**
131→     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
132→     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
133→     */
134→    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
135→        this.filePath = filePath
136→        return this
137→    }
138→
139→    /**
140→     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
141→     */
142→    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
143→        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
144→        return this
145→    }
146→
147→    /**
148→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
149→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
150→     * indenting.
151→     *
152→     * Caution:
153→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
154→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
155→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
156→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
157→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
158→     *
159→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
160→     */
161→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
162→        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
163→
164→        return this
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
169→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
170→     * indenting.
171→     *
172→     * Caution:
173→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
174→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
175→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
176→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
177→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
178→     *
179→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
180→     */
181→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProviders(vararg providers: (() -> Rule)): KtLintAssertThat {
182→        additionalRuleProviders.addAll(
183→            providers.map { RuleProvider(it) },
184→        )
185→
186→        return this
187→    }
188→
189→    /**
190→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s in the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
191→     *
192→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
193→     */
194→    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()
195→
196→    /**
197→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
198→     */
199→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
200→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)
201→
202→    /**
203→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
204→     *
205→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
206→     */
207→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
208→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
209→
210→    /**
211→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] which automatically can be corrected. This is a sugar
212→     * coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
213→     */
214→    public fun hasLintViolation(
215→        line: Int,
216→        col: Int,
217→        detail: String,
218→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolation(line, col, detail)
219→
220→    /**
221→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected. Note that tests
222→     * resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
223→     */
224→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
225→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolations(*expectedErrors)
226→
227→    /**
228→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule which automatically can be
229→     * corrected. This is a sugar coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one
230→     * lint violation.
231→     */
232→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
233→        line: Int,
234→        col: Int,
235→        detail: String,
236→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(line, col, detail)
237→
238→    /**
239→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by an additional rules which can be
240→     * automatically corrected. Note that tests resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to
241→     * comprehend.
242→     */
243→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRule(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
244→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(*expectedErrors)
245→
246→    /**
247→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
248→     */
249→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
253→     */
254→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
255→        line: Int,
256→        col: Int,
257→        detail: String,
258→    ): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(line, col, detail)
259→
260→    /**
261→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s which can not be automatically corrected. Note that
262→     * tests resulting in only one [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
263→     */
264→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): Unit =
265→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(*expectedErrors)
266→
267→    private fun ktLintAssertThatAssertable(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
268→        KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
269→            ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
270→            code = code(),
271→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigProperties.toEditorConfigOverride(),
272→            additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
273→        )
274→
275→    private fun MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>.toEditorConfigOverride() =
276→        if (isEmpty()) {
277→            EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
278→        } else {
279→            EditorConfigOverride.from(*toTypedArray())
280→        }
281→
282→    private fun code() =
283→        if (filePath != null) {
284→            // Create a file containing the code and the given filepath. CodeFile can not be used here as it can not read successfully from
285→            // the Ktlint test file system.
286→            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
287→            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)
288→            Code.fromPath(path)
289→        } else {
290→            Code.fromSnippet(code, kotlinScript)
291→        }
292→
293→    public companion object {
294→        /**
295→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
296→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
297→         */
298→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
299→
300→        /**
301→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
302→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
303→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
304→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
305→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
306→         */
307→
308→        public fun assertThatRule(
309→            provider: () -> Rule,
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
313→
314→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
315→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
316→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
317→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
318→            { code ->
319→                KtLintAssertThat(
320→                    ruleProvider = this,
321→                    code = code,
322→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
323→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
324→                )
325→            }
326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }
353→}
354→
355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
417→     */
418→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()
424→
425→        return this
426→    }
427→
428→    /**
429→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
430→     *
431→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
432→     * formatted.
433→     *
434→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
435→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
436→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
437→     * violated.
438→     */
439→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
440→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
441→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
442→        }
443→
444→        val lintErrors = lint()
445→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
446→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
447→
448→        return this
449→    }
450→
451→    /**
452→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
453→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
454→     */
455→    public fun hasLintViolation(
456→        line: Int,
457→        col: Int,
458→        detail: String,
459→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
460→        hasLintViolations(
461→            LintViolation(
462→                line = line,
463→                col = col,
464→                detail = detail,
465→            ),
466→        )
467→
468→    /**
469→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
470→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
471→     */
472→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
473→        line: Int,
474→        col: Int,
475→        detail: String,
476→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
477→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
478→            LintViolation(
479→                line = line,
480→                col = col,
481→                detail = detail,
482→            ),
483→        )
484→
485→    /**
486→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
487→     */
488→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
489→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
490→
491→        val actualLintViolationFields =
492→            lint()
493→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
494→                .toLintViolationsFields()
495→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
496→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
497→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
498→        return this
499→    }
500→
501→    /**
502→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
503→     * automatically corrected.
504→     */
505→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
506→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
507→
508→        val actualLintViolationFields =
509→            lint()
510→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
511→                .toLintViolationsFields()
512→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
513→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
514→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
515→        return this
516→    }
517→
518→    /**
519→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
520→     */
521→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
522→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
523→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
524→        }
525→
526→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
527→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
528→
529→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
530→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
531→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
532→
533→        return this
534→    }
535→
536→    /**
537→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
538→     */
539→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
540→        line: Int,
541→        col: Int,
542→        detail: String,
543→    ): Unit =
544→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
545→            LintViolation(
546→                line = line,
547→                col = col,
548→                detail = detail,
549→            ),
550→        )
551→
552→    /**
553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
554→     * corrected.
555→     */
556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
557→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
558→
559→        val actualLintViolationFields =
560→            lint()
561→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
562→                .map {
563→                    LintViolationFields(
564→                        line = it.line,
565→                        col = it.col,
566→                        detail = it.detail,
567→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
568→                    )
569→                }.toTypedArray()
570→        val expectedLintViolationFields =
571→            expectedLintViolations
572→                .map {
573→                    LintViolationFields(
574→                        line = it.line,
575→                        col = it.col,
576→                        detail = it.detail,
577→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
578→                    )
579→                }
580→
581→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
582→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
583→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolationFields.toTypedArray())
584→    }
585→
586→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> {
587→        return map {
588→            LintViolationFields(
589→                line = it.line,
590→                col = it.col,
591→                detail = it.detail,
592→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
593→            )
594→        }.toTypedArray()
595→    }
596→
597→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId }
598→
599→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId }
600→
601→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
602→        map {
603→            LintViolationFields(
604→                line = it.line,
605→                col = it.col,
606→                detail = it.detail,
607→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
608→            )
609→        }.toTypedArray()
610→
611→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
612→        val ruleProviders =
613→            setOf(ruleProvider)
614→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
615→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
616→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
617→        val editorConfigOverride =
618→            editorConfigOverride
619→                .enableExperimentalRules()
620→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
621→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
622→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
623→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
624→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
625→        )
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
629→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
630→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
631→        return lintErrors
632→    }
633→
634→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
635→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
636→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
637→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
638→    }
639→
640→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
641→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
642→     */
643→    private data class LintViolationFields(
644→        val line: Int,
645→        val col: Int,
646→        val detail: String,
647→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
648→    )
649→}
650→
651→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
652→    """
653→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
654→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
655→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
656→    """.trimIndent(),
657→)
658→
659→/**
660→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
661→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
662→ */
663→public data class LintViolation(
664→    val line: Int,
665→    val col: Int,
666→    val detail: String,
667→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
668→)
669→
670→/**
671→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
672→ * provided.
673→ */
674→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
675→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
676→        ruleProviders
677→            .asSequence()
678→            .map { ruleProvider ->
679→                ruleProvider
680→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
681→                    .ruleId
682→                    .ruleSetId
683→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
684→            }.distinct()
685→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
686→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
687→            .toList()
688→            .toTypedArray()
689→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
690→}
691→
692→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
693→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
694→

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where property values (and parameter default values) were not correctly indented when wrapped to the next line by the `PropertyWrappingRule` (and `ParameterWrappingRule`).

### Root Cause
The `PropertyWrappingRule` and `ParameterWrappingRule` were designed to provide a "best effort" indentation when a newline is inserted, especially for cases where the standard `IndentationRule` might not be run. However, the `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` function in both rules was using `node.indent()`, which only returns the indentation of the current line. When a newline was inserted before a `CALL_EXPRESSION` or `TYPE_REFERENCE` that exceeded the maximum line length, it was placed on a new line with the same indentation level as the property/parameter declaration itself, instead of being indented by one additional level.

### Fix
I updated the `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` function in both `PropertyWrappingRule.kt` and `ParameterWrappingRule.kt` to use `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)`. This ensures that when a newline is inserted before a child node of a property or parameter, it is correctly indented relative to its parent, consistent with how `requireNewlineAfterLeaf` already behaved.

### Changes
- **PropertyWrappingRule.kt**: Updated `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` to use `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)`.
- **ParameterWrappingRule.kt**: Updated `requireNewlineBeforeLeaf` to use `indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)` for consistency.

### Verification
- Created a reproduction test case that demonstrated the incorrect indentation when the `IndentationRule` was not present.
- Verified that with the fix, the property value is now correctly indented on the next line.
- Verified that all existing unit tests for both `PropertyWrappingRule` and `ParameterWrappingRule` pass successfully.
- Confirmed no new linter errors were introduced in the modified files.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 26→import mu.KotlinLogging
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→
 29→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}

 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()

 33→/**
 34→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a value parameter.
 35→ *
 36→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 37→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 38→ * indentation rule is not run.
 39→ *
 40→ * This rule has many similarities with the [PropertyWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 41→ */
 42→public class ParameterWrappingRule :
 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 45→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 48→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 49→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private var line = 1
 53→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        line = 1
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 72→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 82→
 83→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 84→
 85→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 86→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→        val fromNode =
 88→            node
 89→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 90→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 91→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 92→                ?: node
 93→
 94→        node
 95→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 96→            ?.let { colon ->
 97→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 98→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 99→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
100→                    return
101→                }
102→            }
103→
104→        node
105→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
106→            ?.let { typeReference ->
107→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
108→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                    return
110→                }
111→            }
112→
113→        node
114→            .findChildByType(EQ)
115→            ?.let { equal ->
116→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
117→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
118→                    return
119→                }
120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
146→        node: ASTNode,
147→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→    ) {
150→        emit(
151→            node.startOffset - 1,
152→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
153→            true,
154→        )
155→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
156→        if (autoCorrect) {
157→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
166→    ) {
167→        emit(
168→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
169→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
170→            true,
171→        )
172→        LOGGER.trace {
173→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
174→        }
175→        if (autoCorrect) {
176→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
177→        }
178→    }
179→}
180→
181→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId
